channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The heaviest snowfall in a decade is possible as a wintry blast roils parts of the US
item.description: A blast of snow, ice, wind and plunging temperatures stirred up dangerous travel conditions in parts of the central U.S. on Sunday, as a disruptive winter storm brought the possibility of the “heaviest snowfall in a decade” to some areas.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detroit police officer killed in hit-and-run crash, person of interest at large
item.description: A Detroit police officer was killed on Sunday in a hit-and-run crash while driving home from work, and a person of interest remains at large.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine presses attacks in Russia's Kursk region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he will urge allies to boost Ukraine’s air defenses at a meeting this week in Germany, while both sides said Kyiv’s forces pressed new attacks Sunday in Russia’s Kursk region.
item.dc:creator: VOLODYMYR YURCHUK and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The FBI says the man responsible for New Year's truck attack visited New Orleans twice before
item.description: Shamsud-Din Jabbar, a U.S. citizen from Houston, also traveled to Cairo, Egypt, as well as Ontario, Canada, before the attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: The coroner's office said all the victims died from blunt force injuries. The suspect in the attack, Shamsud-Din Jabbar, was fatally shot in a firefight with police.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you shouldn't charge your phone to 100%, according to experts
item.description: It may be tempting to charge to 100%, but experts say 80% may be enough — and beneficial for your phone. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans mourns victims of truck attack with tearful vigil and celebration of life
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans mourned, wept and danced at a vigil Saturday evening along the famous thoroughfare where a man rammed a pickup truck into a crowd, killing and injuring revelers who were there to celebrate the new year.
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, JACK BROOK, STEPHEN SMITH and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Opening a savings account? Here's what to know
item.description: Annual percentage yields (APYs) on top high-yield savings accounts are currently 4.5% and above, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Millions to get higher Social Security payments from bill Biden is signing
item.description: The bill Biden plans to sign on Sunday will allow roughly 3 million people to receive higher Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In-N-Out opening new restaurants across several states in 2025 and beyond: Here's where
item.description: The burger chain, which does not franchise its restaurants, currently keeps its operations (mostly) to the Western half of the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter 's extended public farewell began Saturday in Georgia, with the 39th U.S. president’s flag-draped casket tracing his long arc from the Depression-era South and family farming business to the pinnacle of American political power and decades as a global humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JEFF AMY and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral has started. Here's what to know
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter began Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A cartoonist has decided to quit her job at the Washington Post after an editor rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing before President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of animals killed in Dallas shopping center fire
item.description: A fire that broke out at a shopping center in Dallas on Friday morning killed more than 500 animals, most of which were small birds, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida mom makes teen son turn himself in for crime
item.description: A 15-year-old boy accused of armed attempted robbery was brought to Florida deputies to be turned in, by his own mother.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IRS reminds taxpayers in 24 disaster area states of 2025 deadlines
item.description: A tax filing extension for residents of two dozen states affected by disasters is coming to an end in 2025, the IRS reminded taxpayers Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Soldier who blew up Tesla at Trump hotel left note saying blast was to be a 'wake up call' for US
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A highly decorated Army soldier who fatally shot himself in a Tesla Cybertruck just before it blew up outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left notes saying the New Year's Day explosion was a stunt to serve as a “wake up call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France. The State Department announced Friday that Blinken would visit Seoul, Tokyo and Paris beginning Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rudy Giuliani insists he's not hiding assets at contempt hearing over $148 million judgment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani seemed to favorably impress a judge with three hours of testimony Friday at a contempt hearing as he insisted he's not hiding assets from lawyers trying to recover a $148 million judgment for two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases heading to auction
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WGN 9

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: Thomas has agreed to follow updated requirements on reporting trips and gifts, including clearer guidelines on hospitality from friends.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former employee charged with child abuse after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s NICU
item.description: A former employee of Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) has been charged with malicious wounding and child abuse after an investigation into babies being found with "unexplainable fractures" at the NICU.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: FBI checks, ethics paperwork threaten to slow down Trump confirmations
item.description: Republican sources on Capitol Hill warn that President-elect Trump’s nominees may be delayed because of paperwork holdups and the slow start of FBI background checks, creating obstacles for the speedy confirmation of Trump’s national security team.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: An orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing so once again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of choking reporter in racially-motivated attack
item.description: "The allegations in this case are very concerning," District Court Deputy DA Victoria Fazzino said. "The people have victim-safety concerns (and) community safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man pushed onto NYC subway tracks recovering while suspect is charged with attempted murder
item.description: A man survived being shoved onto subway tracks ahead of an incoming train in New York City on New Year's Eve and is expected to fully recover, relatives said, while the person accused of pushing him was being held without bail Thursday on attempted murder and assault charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 10 people shot outside New York City nightclub on New Year's Day: NYPD
item.description: At least 10 people were shot outside a Queens nightclub on New Year's Day, according to the New York City Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Multiple dead, injured after car plowed into crowd on Bourbon Street in act of terrorism
item.description: Multiple people are dead after the New Orleans Police Department reports a car may have plowed into a group of people on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, WGN 9

item.title: Firework mortars, gas cannisters stuffed inside Tesla that exploded outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: TY O'NEIL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Islamic State-inspired driver expressed desire to kill before deadly New Orleans rampage, Biden says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A U.S. Army veteran driving a pickup truck that bore the flag of the Islamic State group wrought carnage on New Orleans’ raucous New Year’s celebration, killing 15 people as he steered around a police blockade and slammed into revelers before being shot dead by police.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Death toll in New Orleans crash rises to 15, coroner says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A driver behind the wheel of a pickup truck rammed into a crowd of New Orleans revelers on Bourbon Street early on New Year’s Day, killing at least 15 people in what the FBI is investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: Law enforcement arrested Forrest Kendall Pemberton after he allegedly traveled to Plantation, Fla., in search of AIPAC’s office with several firearms, including an AR-15 rifle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4
